description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a shrimp or prawn, characterized by a long, segmented body with a slightly curved shape. The exoskeleton is a pale pinkish-orange color, with visible segmentation along the abdomen and a pronounced cephalothorax. The head features two prominent eyes on short stalks, long slender antennae, and several pairs of delicate walking legs and swimmerets. The tail is fan-shaped and slightly curled, typical of many decapod crustaceans. The surface appears slightly glossy and semi-hard, with a subtle texture that mimics the natural exoskeleton of a shrimp.
description: A pale pink-orange shrimp with a segmented body, long antennae, and multiple legs, featuring a glossy, semi-hard exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shrimp, with visible legs, antennae, and the segmented body curving slightly upward.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the body, the arrangement of legs, and the narrowness of the shrimp's profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, but with the tail slightly more visible and the antennae extending forward.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the underside, with swimmerets and legs more prominent, and the head and antennae extending outward.
